bids.reasoning: This is the opening bid at t=0, so I should start with my maximum utility offer. All issues have the same utility structure, so valueF gives 1.0 utility on every issue. My top bid gives utility of 0.1*1.0 + 0.2*1.0 + 0.3*1.0 + 0.4*1.0 = 1.0. Starting at the best possible position is standard early-game strategy with no time pressure yet.

The opponent opened with their best offer (all valueA), which gives me minimum utility (0.2 across all issues = 0.2 total). We're very early in the negotiation (t=0.02), so there's no need to concede much. I'll stay close to my top bid but make a small concession on issueA (my least important issue, weight 0.1) to signal willingness to negotiate, while holding firm on issueD (most important, weight 0.4) and issueC (weight 0.3).

The opponent is firmly holding at their maximum utility bid (all valueA) and hasn't conceded at all. It's very early (t=0.04), so I should stay near my top utility while making small concessions. I'll concede slightly on issueA (my least important issue at 0.1 weight) by dropping to valueD, and keep my high-value choices on issueD (most important) and issueC. This keeps my utility high while signaling willingness to negotiate.

The opponent is stubbornly repeating their maximum-utility bid (all valueA) with no concession whatsoever. It's very early (t=0.06), so I should stay near my top utility while making small concessions. I've been conceding on issueA (lowest weight 0.1) which costs me little. I'll continue that pattern and drop issueA to valueC, while holding firm on the high-weight issues (issueD, issueC, issueB). My utility remains very high (~0.95) and I'm signaling willingness to move on low-importance issues.

The opponent has finally changed their offer from all-valueA to something different: issueA: valueF, issueB: valueD, issueC: valueC, issueD: valueA. This gives me utility: 0.1*1.0 + 0.2*0.68 + 0.3*0.52 + 0.4*0.20 = 0.1 + 0.136 + 0.156 + 0.08 = 0.472. That's a signal they care most about issueD (they want valueA there, which is worst for me). I should hold firm on issueD (my highest weight issue) and concede slightly on lower-weight issues. I'll offer issueD: valueF, issueC: valueF, issueB: valueE, issueA: valueB to show some movement while protecting my most important issues.
